differential_diagnoses: Secondary headache due to severe portal hypertension/ascites-related intracranial pressure fluctuations
next_steps: Obtain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the brain with magnetic resonance venography (MRV)
Perform lumbar puncture with measurement of opening pressure and cerebrospinal fluid analysis
Consult ophthalmology for slit-lamp examination and funduscopy to rule out optic nerve edema or ischemia
Check erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR) and C-reactive protein (CRP) levels
Re-evaluate fluid status and hemodynamic stability while titrating vasoactive medications
Perform urgent ophthalmological consultation to evaluate for ischemic optic neuropathy
